input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a known behavioral difference between VSTest and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). When MTP finds zero tests in an assembly, it returns exit code 8, whereas VSTest would return 0. This is a common migration issue.

Let me look at their project files to understand their setup, and I can also invoke the relevant skill for MTP migration guidance.

data.content:
This is a well-known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8** means "zero tests discovered," which MTP treats as an error by default. VSTest returned 0 in this case.

You have two options:

### Option 1 — Environment variable (CI pipeline)

Set this in your CI pipeline:

```
TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8
```

This tells `dotnet test` to treat exit code 8 as success.

### Option 2 — Project-level setting (recommended)

Add the `--ignore-exit-code` option to the project file so it applies everywhere:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- MTP returns exit code 8 when zero tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation).
         Tell dotnet test to treat that as success, matching prior VSTest behavior. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- MTP returns exit code 8 when zero tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation).
         Tell dotnet test to treat that as success, matching prior VSTest behavior. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
The fix works. `dotnet test` now exits with **code 0** even with zero discovered tests.

### Summary

| MTP Exit Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 0 | All tests passed |
| **8** | Zero tests discovered (new in MTP; VSTest returned 0) |

The `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` property tells MTP to treat "no tests found" as success, restoring VSTest behavior. If you have multiple test projects, consider putting this in a `Directory.Build.props` instead.
